recap: To add or subtract fractions use a common denominator; to divide, flip the second fraction and multiply; always simplify and convert mixed numbers to improper fractions first.

questions.question: Work out 3 and 1/2 - 1 and 3/4. Give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.

steps: Convert to improper fractions: 3 and 1/2 = 7/2. 1 and 3/4 = 7/4.

questions.question: Aisha spends 2/5 of her monthly salary on rent and 1/3 on food. She saves the rest. Her monthly salary is £1,800. How much does she save each month? Show all working.

summary: To add or subtract fractions, find the lowest common denominator, convert, then add or subtract numerators only.
To multiply fractions, multiply numerators together and denominators together, then simplify.
To divide fractions, flip the second fraction (reciprocal) then multiply.
Always convert mixed numbers to improper fractions before multiplying or dividing.
